info.name: Property Management APIs - With Specifications
info.description: Complete collection for Seller and Agent property management APIs including property specifications (single-select and multi-select)

variable.description: Property agent activity ID (for show/update/delete). Set from Create response or List.

request.description: Login as seller and get authentication token. Copy the token from response to {{seller_token}} variable.
Login as agent and get authentication token. Copy the token from response to {{agent_token}} variable.
Get all available property specification definitions (filters, types, options). Use this to see what specifications are available when creating/updating properties.
Alias endpoint for getting specification definitions. Same as /specification-filters.
Get all properties for the authenticated seller. Response includes property_specification_definitions.
Get a specific property by ID. Response includes specifications, specification_values, and property_specification_definitions.

{
  "built": "01 01 2020",
  "type": "house",
  "status": "available",
  "bedrooms": 3,

  "address": "123 Main Street",
  "number": "123",
  "city": "Bangkok",
  "state": "Bangkok",
  "country": "Thailand",
  "postal_code": "10110",
  "latitude": 13.7563,
  "longitude": 100.5018,
  "direction": "N",
  "property_initial_owner": "John Smith",
  "property_initial_owner_mobile_number": "0812345678",
  "reference_url": "https://example.com/listing/123",

  "specifications": {
    "floors": "2",
    "bedrooms": "3",
    "bathrooms": "2",
    "parking_spaces": "2"
  },
  "specification_values": {
    "common_facilities": ["Fitness", "Swimming Pool"],
    "furniture": ["Fully Furnished"],
    "air_conditioning": ["All rooms"]
  }
}

request.description:
Create a new property with specifications.

**Single-Select Specifications** (stored in JSON):
- floors: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.)
- bedrooms: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.) or "Studio"
- bathrooms: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.)
- parking_spaces: Exact number ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

**Multi-Select Specifications** (stored in table):
- common_facilities: Array of facilities (["Fitness", "Swimming Pool", "Lawn", etc.])
- furniture: Array of options (["None", "Partially Furnished", "Fully Furnished"])
- air_conditioning: Array of options (["None", "Some rooms", "Almost all rooms", "All rooms"])

request.description: Create a new property without specifications (backward compatible). Specifications can be added later via update endpoint.

Update property specifications. Only include fields you want to update. Other property fields can also be updated in the same request.

**Note**: When updating specification_values for a key, all existing values for that key are deleted and replaced with the new ones.
Create a new property using form-data. Supports all parameters including photos. Add more photos[] entries for multiple images. Date format: dd mm yyyy (e.g., 01 01 2020).
Update a property using form-data. Include only fields to update. Supports photos - new photos are appended. Date format: dd mm yyyy.
Update only specifications. Other property fields remain unchanged.
Update only property specifications without affecting location or other fields.
Update only property location without affecting specs or other fields.

variable.description: The ID of the image to update

request.description: Update property image metadata (alt_text, caption, tag, facing_direction).

variable.description: The ID of the image to delete

request.description: Delete a property image (soft delete).
Delete a property (soft delete).
Get all properties for the authenticated agent (owned or managed). Response includes property_specification_definitions.
Get property status and completion information.

request.description: Create a draft property with any fields. ALL fields are optional - validation only checks format when provided. Missing required database fields use placeholders (bedrooms=0, city='Draft', etc.). No duplicate checking. Returns property with is_draft=true.

Publish a draft property by validating all required fields and setting is_draft=false. Returns 422 with validation errors if required fields are missing or still have placeholder values (type='draft', city='Draft', etc.). Returns 200 with published property on success.
Create a new property. Returns property_id to use in subsequent requests.

request.description: Create a property with specs + location in a single request (recommended). Rejects duplicates with `409 Conflict` (error code `DUPLICATE_PROPERTY`).

Create a new property using form-data. All parameters in one request. Optional: property_initial_owner, property_initial_owner_mobile_number. Photos: use Upload Photos endpoint separately after creation. Date format: Y-m-d (e.g., 2020-01-01).

Update a property using form-data. Include only fields to update. Optional: property_initial_owner, property_initial_owner_mobile_number. Photos: use Upload Photos endpoint separately. Date format: Y-m-d.

request.description:
Create or update property specifications with new specification fields.

**Single-Select Specifications** (stored in JSON):
- floors: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.)
- bedrooms: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.) or "Studio"
- bathrooms: Exact number ("1", "2", "3", etc.)
- parking_spaces: Exact number ("0", "1", "2", etc.)

**Multi-Select Specifications** (stored in table):
- common_facilities: Array of facilities (["Fitness", "Swimming Pool", "Lawn", etc.])
- furniture: Array of options (["None", "Partially Furnished", "Fully Furnished"])
- air_conditioning: Array of options (["None", "Some rooms", "Almost all rooms", "All rooms"])

request.description: Create property specifications without new specification fields (backward compatible). Specifications can be added later by calling this endpoint again with specifications fields.

Update existing property specifications. This endpoint creates if specs don't exist, or updates if they do. Include other property spec fields if you want to update them too.

**Note**: When updating specification_values for a key, all existing values for that key are deleted and replaced with the new ones.
Create or update property location information.

Update property main details (built date, listing type, property_initial_owner, property_initial_owner_mobile_number, etc.) without affecting specs or location. Optional: property_initial_owner, property_initial_owner_mobile_number.
Update only property specifications without affecting location or main property fields. Dedicated endpoint for specs updates.
Update only property location without affecting specs or main property fields. Dedicated endpoint for location updates.

variable.description: The ID of the image to update

request.description: Update property image metadata (alt_text, caption, tag, facing_direction).

variable.description: The ID of the image to delete

request.description: Delete a property image (soft delete).

item.name: 📋 Property Agent Activities
item.description: CRUD for property agent activities: call logs, accept, and deny. Requires agent auth. Property must be owned or managed by the agent.

request.description: Get the list of activities (call, accept, deny) for a property. Returns up to 100 most recent, ordered by occurred_at descending. Response: `{ "success": true, "data": { "activities": [ { "id", "type", "call_outcome", "notes", "reason", "occurred_at", "next_follow_up_at", "agent_name" } ] } }`.
Get a single activity by ID. The activity must belong to the given property; otherwise 404. Set {{activity_id}} from List or Create response.

Create a **call** activity. Use this request for logging a call.

**Fixed values:**
- **type** (required): Must be `call` for this flow.
- **call_outcome** (required when type is `call`): One of — `reached` | `no_answer` | `callback_later` | `wrong_number` | `other` (or any string up to 50 chars).

**Optional:** `occurred_at` (ISO 8601 or date string), `notes` (max 2000), `next_follow_up_at` (date).

Response 201: `{ "success": true, "message": "...", "data": { "activity": { "id", "type", "occurred_at" } } }`. Copy `data.activity.id` to {{activity_id}} for Get/Update/Delete.

Create an **accept** activity (e.g. owner accepted agent representation).

**Fixed values:**
- **type** (required): Must be `accept`.
- **reason** (required when type is `accept` or `deny`): string, max 2000 chars.

**Optional:** `occurred_at`, `notes`.

Create a **deny** activity (e.g. owner declined representation).

**Fixed values:**
- **type** (required): Must be `deny`.
- **reason** (required when type is `accept` or `deny`): string, max 2000 chars.

**Optional:** `occurred_at`, `notes`.

Update an existing activity (partial update). Include only fields to change. Activity must belong to the given property.

**Allowed fields (all optional):**
- **type**: `call` | `accept` | `deny`. If changing to `call`, `call_outcome` becomes required. If changing to `accept` or `deny`, `reason` becomes required.
- **call_outcome**: Required if type is `call`. Values: `reached` | `no_answer` | `callback_later` | `wrong_number` | `other` (or string max 50).
- **reason**: string, max 2000 (required when type is accept/deny).
- **occurred_at**, **notes** (max 2000), **next_follow_up_at**.
Delete a property activity. The activity must belong to the given property; otherwise 404. Set {{activity_id}} from List or Create response.

formdata.description: Optional: N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW

request.description: Upload property photos. Supports file upload or base64 (see other request).
Refresh presigned URLs for S3 images (valid for 1 hour).
Get paginated list of favorited properties for the authenticated buyer.
Add a property to favorites. Idempotent if already favorited.
Remove a property from favorites. Replace 1 with the property_id.
Record or heartbeat a watch session for the property. When called as buyer, stores buyer_id and first_seen_at for time-until-booking analytics.
Returns active watcher count and window_minutes for the property. Use any valid Sanctum token (buyer, seller, agent).
Paginated list of watch sessions for the property. Optional query: buyer_id, per_page.

Returns the competitive set (other property IDs from the search result that contained the booked property). Allowed: booking's buyer, property seller, or assigned agent. Set {{booking_id}} to the numeric booking ID.
Record a click on the property for analytics.
Record a view of the property for analytics.
Record or heartbeat a watch session. As buyer, stores buyer_id and first_seen_at for time-until-booking.

## Complete Workflow Example

### Step 1: Get Available Specifications
1. Run "Get Specification Definitions (Public)" to see all available specifications

### Step 2: Create Property (Seller)
1. Login as Seller
2. Create Property with specifications:
   ```json
   {
     "built": "01 01 2020",
     "type": "house",
     "status": "available",
     "bedrooms": 3,
     "bathrooms": 2,
     "garage": 2,
     "price": 5000000,
     "monthly_rental_price": 25000,
     "description": "Beautiful house",
     "land_size": 200,
     "building_size": 150,
     "house_color": "White",
     "available_from": "01 01 2025",
     "address": "123 Main St",
     "number": "123",
     "city": "Bangkok",
     "state": "Bangkok",
     "country": "Thailand",
     "postal_code": "10110",
     "latitude": 13.7563,
     "longitude": 100.5018,
     "specifications": {
       "floors": "2",
       "bedrooms": "3",
       "bathrooms": "2",
       "parking_spaces": "2"
     },
     "specification_values": {
       "common_facilities": ["Fitness", "Swimming Pool"],
       "furniture": ["Fully Furnished"],
       "air_conditioning": ["All rooms"]
     }
   }
   ```

### Step 3: Update Specifications
1. Update property with new specifications:
   ```json
   {
     "specifications": {
       "floors": "3"
     },
     "specification_values": {
       "common_facilities": ["Fitness", "Swimming Pool", "Lawn"]
     }
   }
   ```

### Step 4: View Property
1. Get property to see all specifications in response
